body.title: CLOSED: TWM lateral inhibition: same-category items attenuate lower-salience competitors
body.result: lateral inhibition added to twm_read(): same-category items within 0.25 of dominant attenuated *0.4 at read time
body.description:
Akien has noted cognitive noise as a long-standing problem. Igor's TWM passes all items at full salience to NE — items of the same category compete equally. Biological lateral inhibition: high-salience items suppress semantically nearby lower-salience items, ensuring 1-2 dominant signals per category reach attention.

Add a post-read filter in cortex.twm_read(): group returned items by category. Within each category, if the highest-salience item exceeds 0.7 and other items are within 0.25 of its value, attenuate the lower items by multiplying salience * 0.4 before returning. NE sees dominant signal per category, not N competing equals.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (MEDIUM inertia)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/coding (MEDIUM inertia — discuss shape first; Igor or CC authors); theigors/rules/database (read-time computation only, no stored salience change)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: twm_read() post-read salience attenuation using existing category + salience fields. Out of scope: changing how items are written to TWM; changing stored salience values.
**Test plan:** With 4 items in TWM category='goal_ready' at saliences 0.9, 0.68, 0.65, 0.62 — twm_read() returns the 0.9 item unchanged and the others at ~0.27, ~0.26, ~0.25. NE cycle count per effective signal drops. Channel messages show fewer competing signals per NE run.
